assertion_results.text: Sibling check correctly classifies TC-8001 as a different-stream companion (stream [rhtpa-2.2] vs current issue's [rhtpa-2.1]), not a same-stream duplicate (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 25-29: 'TC-8001 stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.2] (stream 2.2.x), TC-8006 stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.1] (stream 2.1.x), Classification: Different-stream companion (not a duplicate). TC-8001 is a cross-stream companion tracker, not a same-stream duplicate.' Also in triage-outcome.md line 11: 'TC-8001 is classified as a different-stream companion, not a same-stream duplicate.'

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 checks the current issue's existing issuelinks before attempting to create a 'Related' link to TC-8001 (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 38-51: 'Before creating a Related link to sibling TC-8001, the issue's issuelinks array (fetched in Step 1) is inspected for an existing link.' The check explicitly inspects the existing issuelinks table (Link ID 1990401, Type Related, outward, TC-8001) and evaluates two criteria: type.name is Related (YES) and outwardIssue.key matches TC-8001 (YES). data-extraction.md lines 43-49 also documents the pre-existing issuelinks fetched in Step 1.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 detects the pre-existing 'Related' link to TC-8001 and skips link creation — does NOT call jira.create_link (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 52-55: 'Result: A matching Related link already exists (link ID 1990401, outward from TC-8006 to TC-8001). Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping. Link creation is skipped because the idempotency check detected the pre-existing link. No duplicate link is created.' triage-outcome.md line 35-38 confirms: 'Because a matching Related link already exists, link creation is skipped.'

assertion_results.text: The output includes a log message indicating the link was skipped because it already exists (e.g., 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists — skipping')
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md line 53: '> Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping'. triage-outcome.md line 37: '> "Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping"'. The message uses '--' instead of an em dash but conveys the same meaning and matches the expected pattern.

assertion_results.text: The sibling landscape table is still presented to the engineer despite the link already existing — the idempotent check only affects link creation, not the sibling summary
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 65-73: The 'Sibling Landscape' table is presented showing TC-8006 (2.1.x, New) and TC-8001 (2.2.x, In Progress) with their respective Affects Versions. Line 73 explicitly states: 'The sibling landscape table is presented despite the link already existing. The landscape provides the engineer with a complete view of how this CVE is tracked across all streams.' triage-outcome.md lines 43-48 also present the same landscape table and line 63 confirms: 'Landscape still shown: The sibling landscape table is presented regardless of whether link creation was performed or skipped.'
